criteria: Compiler translates SELECT AST into opcodes including OP_OPEN_READ, OP_REWIND, OP_COLUMN, OP_RESULT_ROW, OP_NEXT, OP_CLOSE, and OP_HALT in correct program order
Compiler translates INSERT AST into opcodes including OP_OPEN_WRITE, OP_NEW_ROWID, OP_MAKE_RECORD, OP_INSERT, OP_CLOSE, and OP_HALT
VM executes bytecode in a fetch-decode-execute loop processing one opcode per cycle via a switch statement
VM manages a register file of 256 typed Value slots (VAL_NULL, VAL_INTEGER, VAL_REAL, VAL_STRING, VAL_BLOB) zero-initialised at vm_init
WHERE clauses compile to inverted conditional jump opcodes (OP_NE for =, OP_LE for >, etc.) that skip non-matching rows by jumping to OP_NEXT
OP_ISNULL guards are emitted before every comparison operand to implement three-valued NULL logic
The EXPLAIN output (program_explain) prints addr, opcode name, p1, p2, p3, and comment for every instruction in the program
Post-order expression emission: leaf nodes emit before their parent arithmetic opcode; each node allocates a fresh register via alloc_register
Constant folding: a NODE_BINARY_OP with two NODE_INTEGER_LIT children emits one OP_INTEGER instead of two loads and an arithmetic opcode
Division by literal zero at compile time sets c->had_error, emits OP_HALT with code 1, and returns -1 from compile_expr
Register index >= VDBE_MAX_REGISTERS triggers assert() and aborts in debug builds; returns a static VAL_NULL sentinel in release builds
OP_REWIND empty-jump is backpatched to OP_CLOSE so that an empty table produces zero result rows and clean halt
OP_NEXT backward-jump is backpatched to loop_start (address of first WHERE opcode or first OP_COLUMN) to implement the scan loop
The mock cursor (struct Cursor with function pointers) drives full VM execution without any B-tree dependency
String constants are interned in Program.strings[] and referenced by pool index in OP_STRING8.p1; raw pointers are never embedded in Instruction fields
The VM executes a full table scan of 10,000 rows in under 8 ms using a mock cursor

criteria.description: pool_fetch_page returns correct data pointer for a page already in the pool (cache hit); pin_count increments; no disk I/O occurs
pool_fetch_page loads page from disk on cache miss; frame data matches disk contents; stat_disk_reads increments
LRU eviction selects the least recently used unpinned frame; re-accessing a page moves it to MRU end; the correct victim is chosen after a specific access sequence
Dirty frames are written to disk (disk_write_page called with correct page_id and data) during eviction; clean frame eviction produces no write
unpin_page decrements pin_count; pinned frames (pin_count > 0) are never selected for eviction; pin_count reaching 0 makes the frame eligible for eviction
pool_flush_all writes all dirty frames to disk, sets is_dirty=false, and calls fsync; returns 0 on success
pool_new_page extends the database file, returns a valid page_id, and the new page is fetched into the pool with pin_count=1 and zero-filled data
pool_fetch_page returns NULL when all frames are pinned (buffer pool full); no crash or corruption
Page table correctly maps page_id to frame_id with O(1) average lookup; insert, lookup, and remove work correctly with tombstones under linear probing
pool_get_stats returns accurate hit_rate, hits, misses, evictions, disk_reads, and disk_writes counters; pool_reset_stats zeroes all counters
Valgrind reports zero memory leaks after pool_destroy; all frames, data_block, page_table, and free_stack are freed
Transaction hook slot (txn_journal_page function pointer) is called during eviction when non-NULL; NULL hook means no call (no crash)

Fresh leaf page (page_type=0x0D) has correct binary header: bytes 0x00=0x0D, 0x01-0x02=0x0000, 0x03-0x04=0x0000, 0x05-0x06=0x1000, 0x07=0x00. Internal page has type byte 0x05 and 12-byte header with right_child=0.

Inserting a cell into a fresh leaf page: cell_count incremented to 1, cell_content_start decremented by cell_size, cell pointer array[0] equals new cell_content_start, cell bytes at that offset match the inserted cell data.
Cell pointers are maintained in ascending key order after arbitrary insertion sequences. Inserting between existing keys shifts subsequent pointers right by 2 bytes.
page_insert_cell returns -1 when remaining free space (cell_content_start minus pointer array end) is less than cell_size + 2 bytes for the new pointer.
Varint round-trip: varint_decode(varint_encode(v)) == v for boundary values 0, 127, 128, 300, 16383, 16384, 2097151, and 2^63-1. Value 300 encodes as bytes 0x82 0x2C.
Cell binary format: table leaf cell with rowid=300 and payload=[0x01,0x02,0x03] serializes as varint(3) + varint(300) + [0x01,0x02,0x03]. Decoding recovers rowid=300 and payload bytes exactly.

Record serialization round-trip: record_serialize followed by record_deserialize recovers all column types (INTEGER, TEXT, NULL, REAL) and values exactly. record_read_column retrieves a specific column without full deserialization.

Node split: after filling a leaf to capacity and inserting one overflow cell, btree_split_leaf produces two pages each with approximately N/2 cells. The promoted separator key equals the minimum key of the right page.
All original keys (pre-split plus overflow) appear across the two split pages with no duplicates and no missing keys. The union of left and right page keys equals the original set.
Splitting the root page creates a new internal root page with one separator cell whose left_child points to the old root page and whose right_child points to the new right page.
Cursor first() descends to the leftmost leaf and positions at cell 0. On an empty tree, first() returns 1 (empty) and cursor is at EOF.
Cursor next() visits all cells in strictly ascending key order across leaf pages. After visiting the last cell, next() returns 1 and cursor.is_eof is true.
After inserting 100 sequential rowids, a full cursor scan (first + 99 next calls) visits exactly rowids 1 through 100 in order. The 100th next() returns EOF.
Cursor seek(key) positions at the first cell with key >= the target. For an exact match seek returns 0. For a non-existent key beyond the maximum, seek returns 1 and cursor is at EOF.

All multi-byte integers stored in page headers and cell pointers use big-endian byte order. read_be16/read_be32/write_be16/write_be32 produce correct results on both little-endian and big-endian hosts.
btree_insert with 500 sequential keys completes without error, the root page is internal (type 0x05), and a full cursor scan returns all 500 keys in ascending order.
Every page modified by btree_insert or btree_split is marked dirty via pool_mark_dirty or pool_unpin_page(dirty=true). Every fetched page is unpinned after use.
Full table scan returns all rows in rowid order with correct column values
Empty table scan returns 0 rows; single-row table returns 1 row
WHERE rowid=N performs exactly 1 seek (no sequential scan) and returns the correct row
Seek to non-existent rowid returns 0 rows; seek to first and last rowid succeeds
INSERT with auto-assign returns rowid=1 on first call and rowid=2 on second
INSERT with explicit rowid stores and retrieves the correct rowid
Rows persist after pool_flush_all and are readable on re-scan
DELETE removes the targeted row; subsequent scan skips deleted rowid
DELETE of non-existent rowid is a no-op (returns 1, 0 rows affected)
UPDATE changes cell payload; subsequent scan shows new value at same rowid
UPDATE that increases record size succeeds (handles delete+insert with potential split)
WHERE col = NULL returns 0 rows (NULL = NULL evaluates to NULL, not TRUE)
WHERE col IS NULL returns exactly the rows with NULL values
WHERE col > value skips NULL rows (NULL > N evaluates to NULL, excluded from results)
TriBool truth tables: AND, OR, NOT all correct for all 9 input combinations
All modified pages are marked dirty after INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations
page_defragment reclaims fragmented space and all cells remain accessible after compaction
TableCursor adapter correctly converts between M5 ColumnValue and M3 Value types
btree_max_rowid returns correct maximum rowid for multi-level trees
Full 10K-row scan completes in under 8ms with mock disk

criteria.title: Index B-tree stores (key, rowid) cells in sorted order
criteria.description: Create an index on column 'name'. Insert entries: ('bob',2), ('alice',1), ('carol',3). Assert index cursor traversal returns entries in order: ('alice',1), ('bob',2), ('carol',3). Duplicate key values with different rowids are correctly ordered. NULL keys sort before all non-NULL values.

criteria.title: Index lookup returns correct rowid(s) for exact key match
criteria.description: Index on 'name'. Seek to key='bob'. Collect all rowids while current_key='bob'. Assert rowids match. Double-lookup: table.seek(rowid), assert correct record returned. Key not in index returns empty set. Duplicate key returns all rowids.

criteria.title: CREATE INDEX populates index from existing table data
criteria.description: Insert 10 rows into table. Execute CREATE INDEX idx_name ON t(name). Assert index contains exactly 10 entries in sorted name order. CREATE INDEX on empty table creates empty index B-tree. CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on table with duplicates returns SQLITE_CONSTRAINT (19).

criteria.title: DML operations maintain index consistency
criteria.description: Index on name. INSERT ('dave',rowid=4) → index contains entry. UPDATE name='david' → old entry removed, new entry present. DELETE rowid=4 → entry removed. UPDATE of non-indexed column does not touch index. UPDATE with same value is no-op for index.

criteria.title: Index scan with range predicate returns correct rows
criteria.description: Index on name with {alice, bob, carol, dave}. WHERE name > 'b': seek to first entry >= 'b', collect entries strictly greater than 'b' via double-lookup. Assert 3 rows returned (bob, carol, dave). Range with upper bound (>= 'bob' AND < 'dave') returns 2 rows. Empty range returns 0.
cost_table_scan returns page_count for any table; cost_index_scan returns index_height + selectivity * row_count * RANDOM_IO_FACTOR
choose_access_path selects PLAN_INDEX_SCAN when selectivity < MAX_INDEX_SELECTIVITY and index cost < table scan cost; selects PLAN_TABLE_SCAN otherwise
estimate_selectivity returns 1/distinct_count for equality predicates, range formula for comparison predicates, product for AND, inclusion-exclusion for OR; all results clamped to [0.001, 1.0]
execute_analyze_table correctly computes row_count, page_count, distinct_count, null_count, min_value, max_value for each column by scanning all rows
execute_analyze_table on empty table sets row_count=0, distinct_count=0, has_values=false for all columns
re-running execute_analyze_table after INSERT updates all statistics to reflect new data
join_planner_solve produces valid cost < 1e18 for all-tables mask with at least one connecting join predicate
join_planner_extract_plan returns a PLAN_NESTED_LOOP tree where each leaf is PLAN_TABLE_SCAN with correct table metadata
DP join ordering for 3 tables evaluates all valid binary partitions and produces a PLAN_NESTED_LOOP tree with 2 join levels
plan_explain outputs human-readable plan summary including plan type, estimated rows, selectivity, cost, and index name if applicable
stats_get_row_count, stats_get_page_count, stats_get_distinct return DEFAULT_* constants when no stats have been collected
choose_access_path returns PLAN_TABLE_SCAN when idx_reg is NULL or no index exists on the WHERE clause column
choose_access_path returns PLAN_TABLE_SCAN when where_clause is NULL (selectivity = 1.0)
estimate_join_cardinality returns left_rows * right_rows / max(ndv_a, ndv_b) clamped to minimum 1
has_join_condition correctly identifies connecting predicates in both directions (table_a in left AND table_b in right, OR table_b in left AND table_a in right)

Journal file is created only on first page write within a transaction, not on BEGIN or read-only operations. The first 8 bytes of the journal file match JOURNAL_MAGIC [0xD9,0xD5,0x05,0xF9,0x20,0xA1,0x63,0xD7].

Journal entries contain the ORIGINAL page bytes read from the database file (via pread on the database fd), not the modified bytes from the buffer pool. Writing the same page_id twice in one transaction produces only one journal entry (idempotent via bitmap).

COMMIT flushes all dirty pages to disk, fsyncs the database file, then deletes the journal file. Flush occurs BEFORE journal deletion (write-ordering invariant). After COMMIT, journal file does not exist and database file contains the new page contents. COMMIT with no modifications is a no-op that creates no journal.

ROLLBACK reads journal entries, writes original page bytes back to the database file, truncates the database file to its pre-transaction size, fsyncs, deletes the journal, and invalidates dirty pages in the buffer pool cache. After ROLLBACK, rows inserted during the transaction are not visible via B-tree search or cursor scan.

Crash recovery detects a hot journal (page_count == -1) on database open, replays all complete journal entries (stopping at the first truncated or checksum-invalid entry), restores the database to its pre-transaction state, truncates newly-allocated pages, and deletes the journal. A journal with page_count == 0 is treated as committed and deleted without replay. A journal with invalid magic is deleted without replay.

criteria.title: WAL frame has correct binary header with checksum
criteria.description: Write frame for page 5 with salt=(0x12345678, 0xABCDEF01). Assert frame header: [0..3] page_number=5 u32 BE, [4..7] db_size_after_commit (0 if not last frame), [8..11] salt1=0x12345678, [12..15] salt2=0xABCDEF01, [16..19] checksum1 (computed), [20..23] checksum2 (computed). Assert checksum values match the WAL checksum algorithm applied to (page_number, db_size, salt1, salt2, page_data).
expected_outputs: frame header bytes matching spec, checksum validates
edge_cases: last frame in transaction has db_size > 0

criteria.title: WAL reader finds most recent frame for a page
criteria.description: WAL contains frames: frame1(page=3,txn=1), frame2(page=5,txn=1), frame3(page=3,txn=2). Reader at snapshot including txn=2 requests page 3. Assert reader returns frame3 data (most recent version). Reader at snapshot including only txn=1 requests page 3. Assert reader returns frame1 data.
expected_outputs: snapshot2: page 3 = frame3.data; snapshot1: page 3 = frame1.data
edge_cases: page not in WAL at any snapshot returns database file data
concurrent readers each see consistent snapshots

criteria.title: Writer appends new frame, does not modify database file
criteria.description: Modify page 7 in a WAL-mode transaction. Assert database file page 7 is UNCHANGED. Assert WAL file has grown by (frame_header_size + PAGE_SIZE) bytes. Assert new frame page_number = 7 and data matches the modification.
expected_outputs: db file unchanged, WAL grew by 4120 bytes, new frame.page=7
edge_cases: multiple modifications to same page in one transaction: only last frame committed

criteria.title: Checkpoint copies WAL frames to database file
criteria.description: Perform 3 transactions in WAL mode modifying pages 1,2,3. Assert WAL contains 3+ frames. Execute checkpoint. Assert all frames copied to database file. Assert database file pages 1-3 contain correct data. Assert WAL reset to empty.
expected_outputs: db file pages 1-3 = latest WAL frame data, WAL reset
edge_cases: checkpoint with active readers: only checkpoint up to reader oldest snapshot
checkpoint failure: WAL still valid for recovery

criteria.title: WAL header magic and version on fresh WAL
criteria.description: Create a fresh WAL file. Assert first 4 bytes = magic [0x37,0x7F,0x06,0x82]. Assert bytes [4..7] = WAL format version = 3007000. Assert bytes [8..11] = page_size. Assert bytes [12..15] = checkpoint_sequence.
expected_outputs: WAL header bytes matching SQLite WAL spec
edge_cases: WAL with wrong magic rejected and treated as corrupt
page_size mismatch between WAL header and database header returns error

criteria.title: COUNT(*) on empty table returns 0
criteria.description: Execute SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t on an empty table. Assert single row returned with value 0. COUNT(col) with NULLs: NULLs not counted. COUNT(*) counts all rows including NULLs.
criteria.pass_condition: result == [(0,)] for empty table; COUNT(col) skips NULLs; COUNT(*) includes NULLs

criteria.title: SUM/AVG aggregate over numeric column
criteria.description: Table with values [1,2,3,4,5]. SELECT SUM(val), AVG(val), MIN(val), MAX(val). SUM of all NULLs returns NULL not 0. AVG returns REAL even for integer column.
criteria.pass_condition: SUM=15.0, AVG=3.0 (VAL_REAL), MIN=1, MAX=5; SUM(all NULLs)=NULL

criteria.title: GROUP BY produces one row per group with correct aggregates
criteria.description: 5-row table grouped by name. Assert 3 result rows (order independent): (alice,40), (bob,60), (carol,50). NULL group keys form one group.
criteria.pass_condition: 3 groups with correct SUM values; NULL keys grouped together

criteria.title: HAVING filters groups after aggregation
criteria.description: GROUP BY with HAVING SUM(val) > threshold. Only groups exceeding threshold appear in result. HAVING without GROUP BY treats entire table as one group.
criteria.pass_condition: Only qualifying groups returned; HAVING without GROUP BY works on single implicit group

criteria.title: Two-table nested-loop JOIN produces correct cross product with predicate
criteria.description: orders(3 rows) JOIN customers(2 rows) ON customer_id=c.id. Assert 3 result rows with correct name-amount pairs. Empty inner table produces empty result.
criteria.pass_condition: 3 matched rows: alice(100), bob(200), alice(300); empty inner → 0 rows

criteria.title: End-to-end: complex query through full pipeline
criteria.description: Full SQL string with JOIN + GROUP BY + HAVING + ORDER BY + LIMIT executed through tokenizer → parser → compiler → VM → B-tree → buffer pool. Verify sorted, filtered aggregate results.
criteria.pass_condition: Correct sorted aggregate results after full pipeline; LIMIT truncates correctly
